full_prompt: Design the integrated structural assembly of an Iran-CanSat Professional-class entry consisting of the main soda-can body plus a deployable two-blade auto-gyro rotor used for stable descent in place of a parachute. The CanSat body fits a 66 mm outer diameter by 115 mm height envelope with total wet mass between 350 g and 500 g (Professional class relaxes the mass cap vs 350 g Standard). The auto-gyro rotor has two hinged blades of length 200 mm (so 400 mm tip-to-tip rotor disc diameter) stowed along the body axis inside a cylindrical top cap of diameter 66 mm and height 220 mm added above the soda-can body (total stowed height 335 mm; only the soda-can body must fit 115 mm, the top-cap rotor is a permitted extension for Professional class per ARI rules). After ejection at approximately 700 m AGL, a spring-release mechanism rotates each blade 90 degrees outward where a mechanical lock engages. The blades are pitched at 4 degrees nose-down. Descent rate is targeted at 8-10 m/s autorotation. Materials: soda-can body is PA12 SLS (E 1.6 GPa, tensile 45 MPa, density 1010 kg/m^3); rotor blades are CFRP solid (UD T300/epoxy, E_long 120 GPa, tensile 1800 MPa, density 1550 kg/m^3); hinge pins are 3 mm AISI 316 stainless (yield 215 MPa). Load cases: (LC1) 20 g axial launch acceleration on 500 g wet mass with rotor stowed; (LC2) rotor deployment kickout: the spring that rotates each blade produces an instantaneous hinge shear of 40 N and an axial tip load of 25 N on the blade root; (LC3) steady autorotation aerodynamic load: each blade carries a distributed lift of 12 N along 200 mm span (3 N centrifugal per blade at nominal 180 rpm autorotation); verify hinge lock-pin shear and blade-root bending; (LC4) ground impact at 10 m/s descent: 10 g equivalent deceleration on the CanSat body base. Pass/fail criteria: (R1) under LC1 launch, body von Mises stress must remain below 22.5 MPa (45 / 2.0 FoS) and rotor-blade stress below 900 MPa (1800 / 2.0); (R2) under LC3 autorotation, hinge-pin shear stress must remain below 107 MPa (215 / 2.0 yield FoS); (R3) blade-root bending stress under LC3 must remain below 900 MPa; (R4) total integrated dry mass (body + rotor + hinges, no electronics or battery) must not exceed 180 g; (R5) rotor deployment mechanism must clear the body skin by at least 2 mm in the fully deployed configuration (collision check, not a stress gate). Thermal, fluid, radiation, and buckling analyses not required. Deliverables: STEP AP242 assembly with body, 2 rotor blades, 2 hinge assemblies, FEA report per load case, deployment kinematics sketch, and mass budget.
short_prompt:
Design the integrated structural assembly of an Iran-CanSat Professional-class entry: soda-can body + deployable two-blade auto-gyro rotor in place of a parachute. 66 mm OD x 115 mm body envelope (soda-can), wet mass 350-500 g; a cylindrical top cap 66 mm x 220 mm is permitted in Professional class to house the stowed rotor (total stowed 335 mm).

Envelope: PA12-SLS body; two CFRP blades (200 mm length, 400 mm tip-to-tip, 4 deg nose-down pitch) stowed axially, spring-release rotates each blade 90 deg outward into a mechanical lock at ejection (~700 m AGL); AISI 316 3 mm hinge pins. Target autorotation 8-10 m/s descent at ~180 rpm.

Factor regime: material FoS 2.0 on body (22.5 MPa) and blades (900 MPa); hinge-pin yield FoS 2.0 (107 MPa); body stress-at-impact same material gate; deployed-rotor body-clearance >= 2 mm (collision check, not stress).

Load cases.
- Launch 20 g axial on 500 g wet with rotor stowed.
- Rotor deployment kickout: 40 N hinge shear + 25 N axial tip load at the blade root.
- Steady autorotation: 12 N distributed lift along each 200 mm blade + 3 N centrifugal per blade; verify hinge lock-pin shear and blade-root bending.
- Ground impact at 10 m/s descent: 10 g equivalent decel on the body base.

requires_non_fea_solver.reason: Rotor deployment kinematics (90 deg blade rotation + lock) verified by rigid-body multibody, not FEA. Rules-compliance only.

prompt.title: Iran ARI CanSat Professional Class — Body + Auto-Gyro Rotor
prompt.brief: Soda-can body + 2-blade hinged auto-gyro for 500 g Professional class, 20 g launch qual + autorotation loads.

load_cases.name: launch_20g
load_cases.application: axial on 500 g wet, rotor stowed

recommended_fea_setup.element_type: solid TET10; beam model for rotor span admissible

notes.needs_rulebook_fetch_reason: ARI Professional-class PDF is registration-gated; top-cap height, exact mass cap, and hinge-pin minimum diameter should be re-checked when the current-year rulebook is obtained. Numeric values above follow typical ARI practice.

exclusions_explained.vibration_analysis: Rotor rotating mass below FEA-grade dynamic concern at 180 rpm (3 Hz).
exclusions_explained.buckling_analysis: Slender members bounded by stress gates.
